payload.query: Because of its strong influence on vote choice, party identification has a voluminous literature. Explain the different conceptualizations of PID (e.g., Fiorina 1981 vs Campbell et al. 1960), why PID is so important, and what you think is the most plausible conceptualization.

articles.title: BILLS FOR REVISION OF J-51 PROGRAM IN COUNCIL
articles.abstract:
The New York City Council approved extensive changes yesterday in a tax-abatement program that permits developers to claim benefits for rehabilitating multifamily housing or converting nonresidential buildings to residential use.

But to the disappointment of a gallery crowded with residents of single-room-occupancy hotels and their supporters, the Council did not approve an amendment that would have eliminated the incentive throughout Manhattan,

The program, known as J-51, provides two types of tax abatement.  Under one, the taxes on a rehabilitated building are frozen for up to 20 years; under the other, a developer may subtract from the frozen tax up to 90 percent of the cost of rehabilitation.
Scheduled to Run Until 1984

Enacted in 1955, the program was intended to induce landlords to rehabilitate cold-water flats and was scheduled to run until 1984.  The Koch administration credits it with having created or upgraded 800,000 apartments in New York City.
articles.snippet:
The New York City Council approved extensive changes yesterday in a tax-abatement program that permits developers to claim benefits for rehabilitating multifamily housing or converting nonresidential buildings to residential use.

But to the disappointment of a gallery crowded with residents of single-room-occupancy hotels and their supporters, the Council did not approve an amendment that would have eliminated the incentive throughout Manhattan,

The program, known as J-51, provides two types of tax abatement.  Under one, the taxes on a rehabilitated building are frozen for up to 20 years; under the other, a developer may subtract from the frozen tax up to 90 percent of the cost of rehabilitation.
Scheduled to Run Until 1984

Enacted in 1955, the program was intended to induce landlords to rehabilitate cold-water flats and was scheduled to run until 1984.  The Koch administration credits it with having created or upgraded 800,000 apartments in New York City.

articles.title: JOE LOUIS, 66, HEAVYWEIGHT KING WHO REIGNED 12 YEARS, IS DEAD
articles.abstract:
Joe Louis, who held the heavyweight boxing championship of the world for almost 12 years and the affection of the American public for most of his adult life, died yesterday of cardiac arrest in Las Vegas, Nev. He was 66 years old.

Mr. Louis, who was champion from 1937 until 1949, collapsed in the bathroom of his home at approximately 9:30 A.M. (P.S.T.). Efforts by Noel Larimer, his personal therapist, and by paramedics to revive Mr. Louis were unsuccessful. He was pronounced dead at Desert Springs Hospital at 10:05 A.M.  His wife, Martha, was with him at the hospital.
Defended Title 25 Times

Slow of foot but redeemingly fast of hands, Joe Louis dominated heavyweight boxing from 1937 to 1948. As world champion he defended his title 25 times, facing all challengers and fighting the best that the countries of the world could offer. In the opinion of many boxing experts, the plain, simple, unobtrusive Brown Bomber - as he was known - with his crushing left jab and hook, was probably the best heavyweight fighter of all time.
articles.snippet:

Efforts by Noel Larimer, his personal therapist, and by paramedics to revive Mr. Louis were unsuccessful. He was pronounced dead at Desert Springs Hospital at 10:05 A.M.  His wife, Martha, was with him at the hospital.
Defended Title 25 Times

Slow of foot but redeemingly fast of hands, Joe Louis dominated heavyweight boxing from 1937 to 1948. As world champion he defended his title 25 times, facing all challengers and fighting the best that the countries of the world could offer. articles.title: STATE BUDGET IMPASSE STIRS CONCERN BY FISCAL AGENCY OVER CITY'S CREDIT
articles.abstract:
The Municipal Assistance Corporation expressed ''extreme concern'' yesterday over the state budget impasse. The corporation's board members said it could endanger New York City's hard-won improvement in its credit rating.

A memorandum to members of the corporation's board at its regular Tuesday meeting said: ''The continued deadlock in Albany is of extreme concern to us for several reasons. The inability to come forth with a budget in a timely fashion not only delays the state's spring financing with costly and disruptive results, but will ultimately hurt the state's credit itself.''
articles.snippet:

articles.title: Senate G.O.P. Faces Pressure to Force ‘Talking Filibuster’ for Voter ID Bill
